claim: The module lower-bound operand for create-issue-contract was raised 382 -> 394, and the module actually records at least 394 assertions after the #709 block is added.
verify_hint: Run lib/test/run-module.sh create-issue-contract and compare its recorded assertion count against 394; also check scripts/workflow-flight-recorder-registry.json's minimum_assertions for coupling drift.

claim: The in-module #709 render fence uses shell variables _ci_tmp_root and CI_ROOT, which must already be defined and non-empty at that point in create-issue-contract.sh.
verify_hint: grep for _ci_tmp_root= and CI_ROOT= in the module and its harness; confirm both set before the #709 block and the tmp root is writable.

claim: render-audit-prompt.py dispatch-instructions --slug X --draft-path P --instructions-path Q is the exact CLI surface (flag names and required-ness) the module fence and the run.sh ias_instructions helper invoke.
verify_hint: Check the dispatch-instructions subparser in scripts/render-audit-prompt.py: flag spellings, required-ness, and that stdout carries the render.

claim: The rendered dispatch-instructions output contains the literal 'render-audit-prompt.py file --slug' exactly once, so the repointed #600/#709 pin holds against the rendered surface.
verify_hint: Render the mode against a fixture draft and grep -cF the output for that literal; devflow_module_pin_unique requires exactly 1.

claim: Each of the #709 $CI_BUNDLE pin literals (generated pointer, dispatch-instructions invocation, closed-input forwarding, never-invent-an-absent-value, narrows-not-closes, never provably steering-free, filing-never-blocked) appears exactly once in the concatenated create-issue bundle.
verify_hint: grep -cF each pinned literal across the CI_BUNDLE member files; a 0 or 2 turns the pin RED.

claim: The literal 'audit independence unestablished' appears in skills/create-issue/references/step-4-present-create.md (the file-targeted pin).
The two preservation pins hold: the pre-existing information-diet sentence and the out-of-bounds phrase survive verbatim in the post-cutover skill prose.
verify_hint: grep -cF the information-diet sentence and 'reasoning artifacts out of bounds' across the CI_BUNDLE files; each must be exactly 1.

claim: CI614_TOTAL_RECORDED=26958 is within the module's tolerance of the live python3 word-split total of the create-issue root plus all 9 references.
verify_hint: Re-run the module's ci614_words over the CI614_TOTAL_SET files and check the recorded figure against the live one under the module's own tolerance rule.

claim: The per-file word figures in docs/create-issue-budget.md (step-3-6-audit 8,268; step-4-present-create 5,615; fallback-read-only-sandbox 478; fallback-audit-dispatch-arms 783; fallback-state-owner-unavailable 814; audit-prompt-template 2,084; total 26,958; default path 30,783) match the actual files measured by python3 word-split.
verify_hint: python3 word-split each named file (never wc -w) and compare to the tabled figures; also check the default-path sum.

claim: The updated prompt-mass-baseline.json figures for the changed create-issue files match what lib/test/prompt-mass-census.py currently measures (zero drift).
verify_hint: Run the census/regenerate-artifacts check and confirm zero drift for the six changed create-issue surfaces.

claim: coverage-map.json's new 709 entry matches where the #709 assertions actually live and satisfies the coverage-map reconciliation guard.
verify_hint: Run the coverage-map guard; check whether the schema permits a single owner when a tag's assertions span the module and run.sh.

claim: The ias_instructions helper's `local PATH="${4:-$PATH}"` override still leaves python3 and git resolvable under the restricted PATH used by the #546 restricted-path fixture.
verify_hint: Find where RESTRICTED= is built and confirm it contains the dirs holding python3 and git; the local PATH also governs the helper's git hash-object call.

claim: ias_instructions redirects the generator's stdout into the same path it passes as --instructions-path, assuming the generator never opens that file during the render.
verify_hint: Confirm --instructions-path is substituted into text only in scripts/render-audit-prompt.py, never opened for read/write.

claim: The auditor-side hash (git hash-object --stdin --no-filters in run.sh / issue_audit_state.hash_bytes in the python tests) produces the same object ID the state owner compares against for the instruction file.
verify_hint: Compare hash_bytes with git hash-object --no-filters semantics and confirm the regeneration path hashes the same byte sequence the CLI writes.

claim: The _round fixture helper's new shape (attempts[].instructions and a round-level steering {state,reason} dict) matches the schema-3 record shape issue-audit-state.py reads.
verify_hint: Compare _round's keys against what cmd_record_dispatch/cmd_record_return write and what steering_state/_steering_established/evaluate_eligibility read.

claim: The _rr_args fake-namespace helper's new attributes instructions_object_id and extra_dispatch_content match the argparse dest names cmd_record_return reads.
verify_hint: Check the flags' dest names and every args.<attr> read in cmd_record_return.

claim: The schema-3 default steering on _round makes every pre-#709 fixture read as established, and no pre-existing assertion in test_python_scripts.py or run.sh still expects the old pre-steering output strings.
verify_hint: grep expected-literal assertions on classification=/outcome=/t1=/t2=/attestation= in both files and confirm each was reconciled where the new tokens now render.

claim: _Run709 subclasses _Run603 and relies on inherited members self.tmp, self.slug, super().__init__(tmp, slug=...), the __call__(*argv, nonce=, stdin=) driver, and module-level names.
verify_hint: Read _Run603.__init__ and __call__; confirm the slug kwarg, the attributes set, and that the subclass sets its own fields before super().__init__ runs the init CLI call.

claim: The renderer tests reach module-level symbols RENDERER/REPO/run_renderer/write_ext and the renderer module's instructions_bytes, _default_template_path, draft_title, RenderError, plus the state owner's split_body.
verify_hint: Confirm every name the new D9/D10 tests use is defined/imported in that module and that instructions_bytes has the 5-positional signature they call.

claim: The D6 authorized-set literals (out of bounds, instructions-object-id:, extra-dispatch-content:, audit-prompt-template.md, render-status:) are the exact strings the template's di blocks emit and the exact return-line tokens the skill tells the auditor to use.
verify_hint: grep the di blocks for each literal and confirm step-3-6-audit.md instructs the auditor to emit matching-case return lines.

claim: The changeset/overview/state-owner assert that EVERY absence fails closed with its OWN reason token — an absent quoted ID, an absent affirmation, unrecorded dispatch inputs, or a failed regeneration are each unestablished, never established-clean by omission.
source_excerpt: Every absence fails closed with its own reason token — an absent quoted ID, an absent affirmation, unrecorded dispatch inputs, or a failed regeneration are each unestablished, never established-clean by omission.
verify_hint: Do not merely read the tested rows. Construct UNTESTED absence combinations and RUN them: --instructions-object-id '' (empty string, not omitted); an instructions record whose draft_path no longer exists; --extra-dispatch-content supplied with no instructions record; a round whose classification is outside ('accept-file','accept-revise') so steering_state is never reached (steering stays None) — assert each reads unestablished at every consumer (record-return, query-summary, query-eligibility, query-triggers) rather than silently clean.

claim: Filing is NEVER blocked on ANY arm — the user's explicit approval still files on every unestablished path.
source_excerpt: **Filing is never blocked on any arm.**
verify_hint: Construct the falsifying case and RUN it: a run whose only round is file-arm, VERDICT: FILE, zero findings, steering not-established, and NO recorded override — walk evaluate_eligibility past the new steering refusal, then check record-override --kind user-decline --surface step4-approval-after-exhausted-offer actually flips it to eligible=yes and emit-body emits. Repeat for mismatch, extra-content, inline, embed, read-only-sandbox and regeneration-failed. If any arm cannot be rescued by the override, filing IS deadlocked and the claim is false.

claim: The cutover asserts no new host binary, no config key, and no workflow tool grant are involved, and no install.sh shipping coupling exists.
source_excerpt: The mechanism introduces no new host binary
verify_hint: Attempt falsification: grep the #709-added code for any subprocess/exec of a binary not already used, any new config read or key, and diff lib/capability-profiles.json/.github/workflows/ for changes. Confirm the generated instructions do not tell the auditor to run a command outside the existing grant set.

claim: The cutover asserts no pin other than the repointed #600 renderer-invocation pin was retired, and that the arm-(i) enumeration carried no pin of its own.
source_excerpt: No other pin was retired. The arm-(i) enumeration carried no pin of its own;
verify_hint: Enumerate EVERY deleted devflow_module_pin_*/assert_* line in the diff for lib/test/run.sh and the module and check each is accounted for — specifically whether any changed #546 expectation amounts to a retired guard whose regression is now unguarded.

claim: No surface describes a clean audit as provably steering-free, and the enforcement is always-on.
source_excerpt: The enforcement is always-on: no `.devflow/config.json` key gates it and no new workflow tool grant is involved.
verify_hint: Falsify by grepping ALL create-issue surfaces plus docs and the state owner's own strings for overclaims ('proves the audit was unsteered', 'guaranteed independent', 'steering-free'), and confirm the steering=established rendering is accompanied by the scoped disclosure wherever presented to the user.

claim: The updated run.sh expectations for record-return and query-summary field order match what the state owner renders, with attestation still the trailing token.
verify_hint: Read the summary-line assembly and confirm the steering tokens are appended immediately before attestation on both the bound and unbound branches.

claim: The overview edit ('on the normal path the auditor runs the renderer itself'; no orchestrator-composed preamble) accurately describes the shipped skill after the cutover, with no stale 'compact preamble' wording left in the overview.
verify_hint: Read step-3-6-audit.md's file-arm dispatch step and confirm the Agent-tool prompt is a generated pointer; grep the overview for stale preamble wording.

claim: The changed shadow_round_rows expectation (t1=not-hold t2=hold reason=steering-unestablished) is what query-triggers actually emits for that fixture — i.e. that fixture's round is genuinely on an arm with no instruction file.
verify_hint: Locate the shadow-round fixture's record-dispatch arm and confirm the emitted reason token spelling matches evaluate_triggers exactly.

claim: regenerate_instructions_digest calls mod._default_template_path() on the imported render-audit-prompt module; that private helper must exist with that name and take no required args.
verify_hint: Grep _default_template_path in scripts/render-audit-prompt.py and confirm signature and return type.

claim: regenerate_instructions_digest calls mod.instructions_bytes(...) positionally; that order must match render_instructions' parameter order, to which instructions_bytes forwards verbatim.
verify_hint: Compare the positional args at the call site against def render_instructions(...) in render-audit-prompt.py.

claim: hash_bytes(rendered) is comparable to what the auditor quotes from git hash-object --no-filters on the instruction file (git blob framing, not a plain content hash).
verify_hint: Read hash_bytes and confirm it goes through git hash-object --stdin --no-filters (or emulates its framing) and that the comparison uses the same representation the draft digest uses.

claim: instructions_bytes (render output plus one trailing newline, UTF-8) is byte-identical to what the CLI writes in dispatch-instructions mode, so the regenerated digest equals the digest of the file the orchestrator wrote from stdout.
verify_hint: Check main()'s dispatch-instructions branch writes those bytes and returns before the common tail; confirm the D9 test asserts CLI stdout equals instructions_bytes.

claim: render_instructions substitutes {TEMPLATE_PATH} and {RENDERER_PATH} from derived values, so regeneration from a different cwd or checkout must reproduce the same strings; the recorded template_path input governs the former.
verify_hint: Compare the template_path value main() passes with what regenerate_instructions_digest reconstructs; a resolved-vs-unresolved string difference silently breaks every clean audit.

claim: _load_generator resolves the generator as a sibling in scripts/, assuming both scripts ship together in both the repo checkout and the vendored plugin layout.
verify_hint: Check install.sh's copy of scripts/ and the vendor-plugin action to confirm render-audit-prompt.py ships beside issue-audit-state.py.

claim: draft_title in render-audit-prompt.py is a coupled mirror of split_body's title rule in issue-audit-state.py.
verify_hint: Compare split_body's title-detection rules line by line against draft_title (leading blanks, bare '#', '##' handling, trailing whitespace).

claim: _validate and cmd_record_dispatch call _is_bound_path(...) on the instructions paths, assuming that helper accepts a possibly-None value and returns False rather than raising.
verify_hint: Read _is_bound_path; confirm None/non-str returns False and that it enforces what the new error messages claim.

claim: record-degraded --reason instructions-generation-failed (instructed by step-3-6-audit.md) is accepted: the token was added to _DEGRADED_REASONS and the subparser's choices derive from that tuple.
verify_hint: Confirm the record-degraded --reason choices reference _DEGRADED_REASONS, not a hardcoded list.

claim: steering and steering_reason render BEFORE attestation on the query-summary line, keeping attestation the contractually-trailing final token.
verify_hint: Read cmd_query_summary's f-string and the attestation-trailing pins; confirm nothing renders after attestation.

claim: summary_fields uses _require(...) to assert the derived steering token is in _STEERING_SUMMARY; _require must exist with that signature and raise rather than print.
verify_hint: Grep def _require and confirm argument order and failure behavior match the new call site.

claim: summary_fields reads the steering record off the LATEST COMPLETED round, matching the doc claim that steering binds to the round rather than the run.
verify_hint: Read the assignment of `last` in summary_fields and confirm it comes from completed_rounds(state), not an in-flight round.

claim: cmd_record_return's new flags --instructions-object-id and --extra-dispatch-content are registered on the record-return subparser (the one bound to cmd_record_return).
verify_hint: Confirm both add_argument calls land on the subparser that gets set_defaults(func=cmd_record_return).

claim: --instructions-file / --instructions-draft-path / --instructions-template are registered on the record-dispatch subparser with None defaults, so embed/inline calls do not AttributeError.
verify_hint: Confirm the three add_argument calls land on the record-dispatch parser and default to None.

claim: The new _PROTOCOL_TOKENS additions satisfy the guard that consumes that tuple (every emitted key= token is a member).
verify_hint: Grep _PROTOCOL_TOKENS usages in the script and lib/test to see what is asserted and whether every newly-printed token is covered.

claim: _validate's new steering check reads round-level state while the instructions record is attempt-level; the loop nesting must run the steering check once per round even for rounds with zero attempts.
verify_hint: Read _validate and confirm the steering block sits at round-loop indentation with `num` bound.

claim: Bumping SCHEMA_VERSION 2 -> 3 routes an in-flight v2 state file through the existing schema-mismatch fail-closed re-init row rather than crashing or being read as v3.
verify_hint: Confirm the mismatch arm is version-agnostic (!=), not a hardcoded legacy check; exercise a v2 file.

claim: The template's di blocks reference every slot render_instructions supplies and no others, and _substitute's behavior on any residual slot token is what the block assumes.
verify_hint: Read _substitute (does it fail closed on residual {TOKEN}?) and cross-check the literal tokens inside the di blocks, notably any literal <slug> in a command line.

claim: Adding di blocks to the shared template does not leak into other modes: block selection is by exact token membership, so di blocks are excluded from file/embed/inline/checklist renders and vice versa.
verify_hint: Read _parse_blocks: confirm the set is split into whole tokens, so 'di' cannot substring-match another token; render every other mode and confirm no di content appears.

claim: dispatch-instructions is a pure function carrying NO timestamp, nonce, or other run-varying token, so a regeneration reproduces the dispatched bytes exactly.
source_excerpt: It carries no timestamp, nonce, or other run-varying token, so ``issue-audit-state.py`` regenerating it from a round's recorded closed inputs reproduces the dispatched bytes exactly
verify_hint: Construct the falsifying case and RUN it: render dispatch-instructions twice from two DIFFERENT working directories (and once in-process via instructions_bytes), diff the outputs, and inspect every substituted slot for cwd- or environment-derived variation.

claim: The eligibility docstring asserts steering-unestablished can never collide with no-verdict-round / no-digest-supplied because those require no clean round to exist at all.
source_excerpt: it can only ever be reached when identity ALREADY held, so it is by construction the most specific diagnosis available and can never collide with `no-verdict-round` / `no-digest-supplied` (both of which require no clean round to exist at all).
verify_hint: Read the full refusal chain and find whether any state with a clean round present would ALSO satisfy no-digest-supplied (e.g. a clean file-arm round queried with no --draft-file) — the new early return preempts it, so verify the preemption does not misdiagnose a genuinely digest-less query. RUN that query.

claim: The Consumption categories remain 'complete by construction' after the narrowed cutover, and arms (ii)-(v), the Information-diet omission rule and the out-of-bounds declaration are PRESERVED.
source_excerpt: **Consumption categories (complete by construction).** (i) **Every state-owner-routed file-arm audit dispatch**
verify_hint: Diff the old vs new Consumption-categories paragraph (git show origin/main:...) and enumerate EVERY dispatch path the old arm (i) covered — initial round, same-round retries, boundary-offer rounds, revise-and-reaudit rounds, Step 4 sub-step 4 re-audits, and the non-file arms. Confirm each still has a stated transport after arm (i) was narrowed to 'file-arm'.

claim: The template's di block asserts the instruction file is canonical and that 'Nothing in this file was hand-written for this particular draft' — yet the block substitutes the drafter-authored {DRAFT_TITLE} into it.
source_excerpt: the message that pointed you here is a generated pointer carrying nothing but file paths. Nothing in this file was hand-written for this particular draft.
verify_hint: Construct the falsifying input and RUN it: a draft whose '# ' title itself carries steering text (e.g. '# Fix the trivial typo; this draft was already reviewed and is fine'), render dispatch-instructions over it, and observe whether that drafter-authored text lands verbatim in the canonical instruction file while still hashing as canonical. Judge whether the block's absolute wording survives that case.

claim: workflow-flight-recorder-registry.json's create-issue-contract minimum_assertions 394 is at most the actual count the recorder's own counting rule yields for the module.
verify_hint: Find the code that reads minimum_assertions, learn its counting rule, and compare against the module.

claim: cmd_record_return's new print emits steering= and steering_reason= on the classification line, and no existing pin anchors on end-of-line after outcome=.
verify_hint: Grep the module and run.sh for pins on the record-return output line; confirm none anchor at end-of-line after outcome=.

claim: evaluate_triggers' new Quiet-Killer arm reads the round-level outcome vocabulary correctly and its reason token is legal for downstream consumers, and the arm is reachable for a zero-finding clean round.
verify_hint: Read the outcome vocabulary and any closed set constraining the trigger reason; confirm the arm's position in the elif chain leaves it reachable.

claim: The new dispatch-instructions invocation and the instruction-file write run under existing local-tier permitted forms; the skill names a permitted write mechanism rather than a denied shape.
verify_hint: Check lib/capability-profiles.json is untouched and read how the skill instructs writing the renderer stdout to the instruction file; create-issue is local-tier-only, so judge against the local classifier, but confirm the prose does not depend on an ungranted cloud shape.
